manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: ingratitude, bonkers, fruition, ambivalence, veracity, affability, adaptation, discernment, richness.
Your opponent's team words are: knowledge, glorification, dissolution, unreality, urge, endlessness, anomaly, pragmatism.
Distractor words are: incompatibility, gladness, proliferation, nationalization, subjugation, impulsivity, socialization.
The kill words are: willingness.

message.reasoning:
We need to produce a clue word and list of target words (our team words) that our clue relates to. Must be single-word clue not any of the listed words. Should be semantically related. We can pick any subset (including all). Need to avoid clue being any of the words in any list (team, opponent, distractor, kill). The clue must not contain parts of them (i.e., cannot be substring?). Probably avoid any overlap.

Our team words: ingratitude, bonbons? actually bonkers, fruition, ambivalence, veracity, affability, adaptation, discern... richness.

We need to think of a clue that links multiple. Possibly "growth" could relate to fruition, richness, adaptation, discernment? maybe too vague. "Honesty" for veracity, affability? Not exactly. "Balance" could link ambivalence, adaptation, discernment? Hmm.

Goal: maximize number of targets but ensure partner can guess correctly. Also avoid opponent/distractor clues.

Let's try to pick 3 or 4. Maybe clue "development" relates to adaptation, fruition, richness? Also maybe "maturity". Maturity relates to fruition (fruit ready), richness (mature wine), adaptation (mature mind), discenement? Could be.

Check if "maturity" contains parts of any words? No.

So we could output:

CLUE: Maturity
TARGETS: fruition, richness, adaptation

Check if any opponent/distractor words associated? Not too strongly maybe.

But "maturity" could also hint at "veracity"? Not really.

Alternatively "sincerity" could link veracity, affability, discen... but not.

"Complexity" maybe for ambivalence, discernment, adaptation? Not.

Let's choose "Maturity" with three targets.

Make sure clue is single word, capitalized? Format.

Thus final answer
